note_data.title: Trigonometry
keyPoints: **Sine rule**: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C applies to any triangle
**Cosine rule**: a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A applies to any triangle
**Area formula**: Area = ½ab sin C for any triangle with two sides and included angle
**Pythagorean identity**: sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 for all values of θ
**Tangent identity**: tan θ = sin θ/cos θ for all values where cos θ ≠ 0
**Complementary angles**: sin(90° - θ) = cos θ and cos(90° - θ) = sin θ
**Negative angles**: sin(-θ) = -sin θ, cos(-θ) = cos θ, tan(-θ) = -tan θ
**Angle sum formulas**: sin(A ± B) = sin A cos B ± cos A sin B
**Double angle formulas**: sin 2θ = 2 sin θ cos θ, cos 2θ = cos²θ - sin²θ

definitions.definition: In any triangle ABC, a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C where a, b, c are sides opposite to angles A, B, C respectively
In any triangle ABC, a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A where a is the side opposite to angle A
An equation involving trigonometric functions that is true for all permissible values of the variable
Two angles whose sum is 90 degrees

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Show each step clearly using algebraic manipulation or known identities
Calculate the exact value showing all working steps
Find all solutions in the given range and show clear working

modelAnswer.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 30°, side b = 8 cm and side c = 6 cm. Find the area of the triangle.
modelAnswer.answer:
Area = ½bc sin A
Area = ½ × 8 × 6 × sin 30°
Area = ½ × 8 × 6 × ½
Area = 12 cm²
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct formula Area = ½bc sin A
Mark 2: Correct substitution with sin 30° = ½
Mark 3: Correct final answer 12 cm² with units

commonMistakes: Never use degrees and radians together in the same calculation
Students often use sine rule when cosine rule is required - check what information is given
Always check if angle is acute or obtuse when using sine rule - sin θ = sin(180° - θ)
Never leave answers as decimals when exact values are expected
Students forget to use the correct angle in area formula - must be the included angle between two given sides
examinerTips: Trigonometry appears in every Paper 2 - usually 15-20 marks total
Always draw a clear labelled diagram for triangle problems
Learn exact values: sin 30° = ½, sin 45° = √2/2, sin 60° = √3/2
Show each step when proving identities - examiners award method marks
formulas: a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Triangle ABC with sides a, b, c opposite to angles A, B, C respectively. Label all given information clearly and mark the angle being used in calculations.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q8 (6 marks) — they asked students to find the angle of elevation from a point 50m from the base of a tower to the top, given the tower height was 35m
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (4 marks) — candidates had to use sine rule to find the third side of a triangle given two sides and a non-included angle
This topic was examined in M/J 2022 P2 Q7 (5 marks) — students needed to solve a right-angled triangle problem involving bearings and find the distance between two ships
This topic featured in O/N 2023 P2 Q9 (7 marks) — they tested cosine rule application to find an angle in a triangle where all three sides were given

workedExample.question: A ladder of length 8m leans against a vertical wall. The foot of the ladder is 3m from the base of the wall. Calculate the angle the ladder makes with the ground. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Draw a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse = 8m (ladder), adjacent side = 3m (ground distance)
Step 2: Identify which trigonometric ratio to use. We have adjacent and hypotenuse, so use cosine
Step 3: Write the equation: cos(θ) = adjacent/hypotenuse = 3/8 = 0.375
Step 4: Find the angle: θ = cos⁻¹(0.375) = 67.97° ≈ 68.0°
workedExample.answer: The angle the ladder makes with the ground is 68.0° (to 1 decimal place)
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct diagram/identification of triangle, 1 mark for choosing cosine ratio, 1 mark for correct substitution, 1 mark for final answer to appropriate accuracy

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused sine and cosine ratios in right-angled triangles' — always check whether you have opposite/adjacent relative to the angle you're finding
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to convert their calculator from radians to degrees' — always verify your angle mode before calculating inverse trigonometric functions

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates lost marks by not rounding to the required number of decimal places or significant figures' — read the question carefully for accuracy requirements and round only the final answer
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw and label a right-angled triangle showing: the right angle marked with a square symbol, the angle being calculated marked with θ or the given letter, all three sides clearly labeled (hypotenuse opposite the right angle, opposite side facing the angle θ, adjacent side next to angle θ), and given measurements written on the appropriate sides. For non-right triangles, draw any triangle with all sides and angles clearly labeled using letters (a,b,c for sides opposite angles A,B,C respectively).
note_data.quickRecap: SOH-CAH-TOA applies only to right-angled triangles: Sin = Opposite/Hypotenuse, Cos = Adjacent/Hypotenuse, Tan = Opposite/Adjacent. For any triangle, use sine rule a/sinA = b/sinB = c/sinC or cosine rule a² = b² + c² - 2bc×cosA. Always check your calculator is in degree mode. Round only your final answer to the accuracy specified in the question.
